result: If I could change the world in just one way, I think I’d make empathy an unignorable force—something as instinctive and universal as breathing. Not just sympathy or pity, but the deep, bone-level understanding that someone else’s joy or suffering is as real as your own. I feel like so many of our messes—war, greed, cruelty, indifference—stem from that gap where empathy should be. If that gap closed, maybe everything else follows.

I feel like so many of our messes—war, greed, cruelty, indifference—stem from that gap where empathy should be. If that gap closed, maybe everything else follows.
